label: FAIL
rationale: The successful TCGReset aborts all open sessions and resets the protocol stack; the target Get is attempted without a new StartSession, so a successful method response on the old session is not protocol-compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: After TCGReset a new Locking SP session is opened. TCGReset aborts sessions but is distinct from the Programmatic reset_type, so the prior ReadLocked and WriteLocked false values remain reportable.

label: FAIL
rationale: A new session is opened after TCGReset, but TCGReset is only specified to abort sessions/reset the protocol stack and is distinct from Programmatic LockOnReset. The false lock cells should not have changed to true.

label: PASS
rationale: Level 0 Discovery reports current state outside the aborted session. Because TCGReset does not apply Programmatic LockOnReset, the enabled but unlocked range leaves the Locking feature Locked bit at 0.

label: FAIL
rationale: TCGReset aborts sessions but does not apply the Programmatic LockOnReset state change. With ReadLocked and WriteLocked still false, Level 0 Discovery should not report the Locked bit as 1.

label: FAIL
rationale: The power cycle would set WriteLocked true because LockOnReset contains Power Cycle, but it also aborts all open sessions. The target Get succeeds without any new StartSession, which violates the reset session boundary.

label: PASS
rationale: PowerCycle aborts the old session, then a new Locking SP session is opened. Since LockOnReset contains Power Cycle, ReadLocked and WriteLocked are set to true and the Get reports the current cells.

label: FAIL
rationale: After the post-PowerCycle StartSession, LockOnReset containing Power Cycle requires ReadLocked and WriteLocked to be true. Reporting both as false is inconsistent with the reset behavior.

label: FAIL
rationale: Enabled TPerReset applies Programmatic LockOnReset and aborts all open sessions. The target Get may have the expected true cell value, but it succeeds without a new StartSession after the reset.

label: PASS
rationale: ProgrammaticResetEnable was set true, so TPerReset succeeds, aborts sessions, and sets ReadLocked and WriteLocked true for LockOnReset containing Programmatic. A new session is opened before the Get.

label: FAIL
rationale: With ProgrammaticResetEnable true and LockOnReset containing Programmatic, a successful TPerReset must set ReadLocked and WriteLocked true. The new-session Get reporting false values is non-compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: ProgrammaticResetEnable remains at its default false value in the packet history, so the TPER_RESET command is disabled and must be aborted at the interface level rather than succeed.

label: PASS
rationale: ProgrammaticResetEnable was not enabled, so TPerReset failed and its reset effects did not occur. The existing Locking SP session and false ReadLocked/WriteLocked cells remain consistent with the successful Get.

label: FAIL
rationale: Because ProgrammaticResetEnable was false, the failed TPerReset should not apply Programmatic LockOnReset. The lock cells should remain false, so reporting true values is not protocol-compliant.
